heroTitle: Safe ops transition. Replace your dependent stack in three phases—no cliff, no lock-in.
heroLead: The Nexa Infrastructure Audit maps your current stack, your risk, and your first safe replacement lane. Any industry. Any niche. Individuals or teams. Then the Nexa platform carries you through a clear, psychology-aware journey from audit to live replacement.
funnelSubline: Audit → Design → Replace. Your stack, your pace, your control.
problemHeadline: Your stack depends on tools you don't control.
problemBody: Every industry—and every individual operator—has a dependent stack: the tools, workflows, and integrations that have to work. Replacing them in one shot is risky. We run a three-phase transition so you keep mental clarity and operational safety at every step.
phaseOneBody: We map what you run today: tools, hidden manual work, and where automation or replacement could create risk. You get a deployment map and the first lane worth changing—no commitment to replace everything.
phaseTwoBody: Control plane, data boundaries, and human-override points are defined before anything goes live. Copy and flows are tuned for your niche and for the mental load of change—so your team and your users stay oriented.
phaseThreeBody: We transition one dependent piece at a time through our commercial SaaS. Safe ops, transitional replacement, and a clear user journey—so you move off legacy without a cliff.
journeySteps: Receive your map and first recommended lane in 48 hours.
Join our SaaS for phase-by-phase replacement with support and design.
auditCtaBody: One form. We return a fit assessment and the first safe replacement lane. Commercial SaaS access comes after—only if the audit direction works for you.
saasCtaBody: Our safe-ops platform guides you through the three phases with the right copy, design, and user journey for your industry. Transitional replacement—not big-bang.
saasCtaButton: Start the audit to get access
intakeBody: Tell us what you run today. We return a deployment map, risk notes, and the first automation lane worth shipping.
successBody: Your company profile is in the queue. Nexa will return a fit assessment and deployment direction within 48 hours.
proofBody: Collections, compliance, handoff queues, and document-heavy operations need structure, auditability, and human override paths. Nexa is built around those constraints.

systemCards.title: Revenue Infrastructure
systemCards.body: Lead capture, qualification, follow-up, quoting, payment links, and collections handoff in one operating surface.

systemCards.title: Backoffice Automation
systemCards.body: Document routing, intake classification, customer operations, status updates, and repeatable internal workflows.

systemCards.title: Governed AI Layers
systemCards.body: Human override, audit logs, constrained automation, and system boundaries designed before production launch.

deliverySteps.title: Assess
deliverySteps.body: Map current tools, hidden manual work, and where risk appears when automation touches customers or money.

deliverySteps.title: Architect
deliverySteps.body: Define control plane, data boundaries, review gates, and Vercel-facing product surfaces.

deliverySteps.title: Ship
deliverySteps.body: Launch the first working lane fast: landing page, intake flow, workflow engine, or operator dashboard.

deliverySteps.title: Stabilize
deliverySteps.body: Add monitoring, escalation paths, incident handling, and the next automations without losing control.

rails: Consent-first automation and verified user paths
Manual override for high-risk steps and customer-facing actions
Separation between frontend speed and sensitive backend logic
Security and ethics review for systems that touch money or legal exposure
footerNote: Canadian-focused deployments with sovereign operating discipline and no dependency on generic SaaS lock-in.
launchSurfaceTitle: Ship the first serious page, then the system behind it.
launchSurfaceBody: This landing page is built for Vercel deployment, but the product story is bigger: fast acquisition, clear trust framing, and a route into governed automation.
formFootnote: Endpoints can be overridden at build time with NEXT_PUBLIC_ACCESS_ENDPOINT, NEXT_PUBLIC_LEAD_ENDPOINT, and NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT.
